messages.subject: [R-sig-Debian] problems apt-getting from CRAN
messages.body_plain:
Le Jeudi 15 F?vrier 2007 08:52, Johannes Ranke a ?crit?:
> * Dirk Eddelbuettel <edd at debian.org> [070215 05:20]:
> > On 14 February 2007 at 15:29, Federico Calboli wrote:
> > | Hi there,
> > |
> > | I hope that's not been asked ad nauseam (but I could not find it in the
> > | archives...) but, if I put a CRAN mirror in my sources.list and I try
> > | to install the latest R version for 'testing' I get the warning that
> > | the packages cannot be autenticated.
> > |
> > | I tried to figure out how to get a GPG key from the mirror I'm using
> > | (the austrian one) in case that's the solution, but fat chance! apt-get
> > | update does not return any error anyawy, i.e. does not warn me of any
> > | missing GPG key from the CRAN mirror.
> > |
> > | Any suggestion would be apprecciated.
> >
> > I'd guess it's simply missing.  Try wiki.debian.org for info on how to
> > _create_ such a key, and then convince someone at CRAN to create one.
>
> OK, I learned how to create and use an archive key by doing it.
> Currently this key is only signed by me, but better then no signing at
> all, I believe.
>
> For details, see the updated README file, section SECURE APT. The
> key ID is 381BA480.
>
> The signed Release files and the README will be picked up by CRAN within
> 12 hours, and then propagate to the mirrors.
>
> Johannes

I did the same for the Ubuntu packages. They're signed with by key E2A11821. 
The README has been updated accordingly.

HTH

-- 
  Vincent Goulet, Professeur agr??g??
  ??cole d'actuariat
  Universit?? Laval, Qu??bec 
  Vincent.Goulet at act.ulaval.ca   http://vgoulet.act.ulaval.ca

> > > | to install the latest R version for 'testing' I get the warning that
> > > | the packages cannot be autenticated.
> > > |
> > > | I tried to figure out how to get a GPG key from the mirror I'm using
> > > | (the austrian one) in case that's the solution, but fat chance! apt-get
> > > | update does not return any error anyawy, i.e. does not warn me of any
> > > | missing GPG key from the CRAN mirror.
> > > |

> > > _create_ such a key, and then convince someone at CRAN to create one.

> > all, I believe.
> >
> > For details, see the updated README file, section SECURE APT. The
> > key ID is 381BA480.
> >
> > The signed Release files and the README will be picked up by CRAN within
> > 12 hours, and then propagate to the mirrors.
> >
> > Johannes
> 
> I did the same for the Ubuntu packages. They're signed with by key E2A11821. 
> The README has been updated accordingly.

Nice. When I started to use a signed release file, at first I got error
messages from apt due to the very slim Release file produced when
calling apt-ftparchive release without a config file, so I created
config files for sarge and etch backports. If you want to see my
repository maintenance scripts/config, see

	http://www.uft.uni-bremen.de/chemie/ranke/r-cran

This seems to do the job. Suggestions for improvements/corrections are
always welcome.

Johannes

messages.subject: [R-sig-Debian] [R] Installing R on Ubuntu 6.10 via apt-get
messages.subject_clean: Installing R on Ubuntu 6.10 via apt-get
messages.body_plain:
[Redirected to r-sig-debian where this belongs]

Le Vendredi 9 Mars 2007 06:43, Antonio Olinto a ?crit?:
> Hi
>
> I'm using Linux Ubuntu 6.10 on a Pentium D 2.8.
>
> Well, following http://cran.r-project.org/bin/linux/ubuntu/README
>
> I wrote in the sources.list
>
> # R
> deb http://CRAN.R-project.org/bin/linux/ubuntu edgy/
> deb http://www.vps.fmvz.usp.br/CRAN/bin/linux/ubuntu edgy/
>
> But after type "apt-get update" I got
>
> Falha ao baixar
> http://www.vps.fmvz.usp.br/CRAN/bin/linux/ubuntu/edgy/Release Unable to
> find expected entry Packages in Meta-index file (malformed Release file?)
> Falha ao baixar http://CRAN.R-project.org/bin/linux/ubuntu/edgy/Release
> Unable to find expected entry Packages in Meta-index file (malformed
> Release file?)
> W: Conflicting distribution: http://www.vps.fmvz.usp.br edgy/ Release
> (expected edgy but got )
> W: Conflicting distribution: http://CRAN.R-project.org edgy/ Release
> (expected edgy but got )
>
> (PS. Falha ao baixar = Fail to download)
>
> The key of Vincent Goulet seems to be OK.
>
> Am I doing something wrong or there's really a problem with the Release
> file?

Johannes,

Is this the kind of problem you were referring to in your Wednesday message? 
Honestly, I did not understand the " I got error messages from apt due to the 
very slim Release file produced when calling apt-ftparchive release without a 
config file" part. Could you please explain in some more detail so I can fix 
things.

Antonio: I'll keep you posted when the problem is fixed.

-- 
  Vincent Goulet, Associate Professor
  ??cole d'actuariat
  Universit?? Laval, Qu??bec 
  Vincent.Goulet at act.ulaval.ca   http://vgoulet.act.ulaval.ca

>

messages.subject: [R-sig-Debian] [R] Installing R on Ubuntu 6.10 via apt-get
messages.subject_clean: Installing R on Ubuntu 6.10 via apt-get
messages.body_plain:
Hi,

* Vincent Goulet <vincent.goulet at act.ulaval.ca> [070309 19:20]:
> [Redirected to r-sig-debian where this belongs]
> 
> Le Vendredi 9 Mars 2007 06:43, Antonio Olinto a ?crit?:
> > Hi
> >
> > I'm using Linux Ubuntu 6.10 on a Pentium D 2.8.
> >
> > Well, following http://cran.r-project.org/bin/linux/ubuntu/README
> >
> > I wrote in the sources.list
> >
> > # R
> > deb http://CRAN.R-project.org/bin/linux/ubuntu edgy/
> > deb http://www.vps.fmvz.usp.br/CRAN/bin/linux/ubuntu edgy/
> >
> > But after type "apt-get update" I got
> >
> > Falha ao baixar
> > http://www.vps.fmvz.usp.br/CRAN/bin/linux/ubuntu/edgy/Release Unable to
> > find expected entry Packages in Meta-index file (malformed Release file?)
> > Falha ao baixar http://CRAN.R-project.org/bin/linux/ubuntu/edgy/Release
> > Unable to find expected entry Packages in Meta-index file (malformed
> > Release file?)
> > W: Conflicting distribution: http://www.vps.fmvz.usp.br edgy/ Release
> > (expected edgy but got )

This means that in the Release file the distribution is not specified.

> > W: Conflicting distribution: http://CRAN.R-project.org edgy/ Release
> > (expected edgy but got )
> >
> > (PS. Falha ao baixar = Fail to download)
> >
> > The key of Vincent Goulet seems to be OK.
> >
> > Am I doing something wrong or there's really a problem with the Release
> > file?
> 
> Johannes,
> 
> Is this the kind of problem you were referring to in your Wednesday message? 
> Honestly, I did not understand the " I got error messages from apt due to the 
> very slim Release file produced when calling apt-ftparchive release without a 
> config file" part. Could you please explain in some more detail so I can fix 
> things.

If you call "apt-ftparchive release", then you get a release file just
with checksums, but with no further information about the repository.
To fix this, you can specify a config file. For example, in my
repository maintenance script

	http://www.uft.uni-bremen.de/chemie/ranke/r-cran/update_etch.sh

I call apt-ftparchive like this

	apt-ftparchive -c apt-ftparchive_etch.conf release etch

which makes apt-ftparchive use the config file apt-ftparchive_etch.conf
where some details about the repository are given. If you produce your 
Release file this way, the problem should go away.

I also included some more information in this config file, which means
that I can produce the Packages.gz and Sources.gz files by calling

	apt-ftparchive generate apt-ftparchive_etch.conf

which is the first line in the script referred to above.

Best,

Johannes
	
> 
> Antonio: I'll keep you posted when the problem is fixed.
> 
> -- 
>   Vincent Goulet, Associate Professor
>   ??cole d'actuariat
>   Universit?? Laval, Qu??bec 
>   Vincent.Goulet at act.ulaval.ca   http://vgoulet.act.ulaval.ca

-- 
Dr. Johannes Ranke                 jranke at uni-bremen.de     Key ID: F649AF90
UFT Bremen, Leobenerstr. 1         +49 421 218 8971
D-28359 Bremen                     http://www.uft.uni-bremen.de/chemie/ranke

start propagating to CRAN in about half an hour.

Antonio and David: could you please retry installing the packages and  
let me know how it goes?

Sorry for the inconvenience.

---

messages.subject: [R-sig-Debian] getting the Debian pgp key --more detail
messages.subject_clean: getting the Debian pgp key --more detail
messages.body_plain:
When I execute the command

gpg --keyserver subkeys.pgp.net --recv-key 381BA480

as specified in the README, the keyserver times out. I tried
several key servers and same thing occurs. When I use the
the email or brower options on the keyservers, it responds
that the key does not not exist.

I'm not sure how to get this key from the CRAN mirrors as
Dirk suggested.  I tried the fhcrc and sfu servers.

Anne

messages.subject: [R-sig-Debian] getting the Debian pgp key
messages.body_plain:
* Dirk Eddelbuettel <edd at debian.org> [070320 03:30]:
> 
> On 19 March 2007 at 15:45, Anne York wrote:
> | I've tried to obtain the public key as described in the 
> | README file in bin/linux/Debian on CRAN. The server 
> | specified in the README times out. The mit public key 
> | server responds that there is no such key.
> 
> Based on the last messages by Johannes and Vincent, I would expect these
> files to be on all CRAN mirrors. Maybe you can just try a different one?
 
I am not sure if it makes sense to put the keys on the mirrors. If they
should be compromised, the keys could be compromised, too, so I would
think it's better to only provide them from an independent source as the 
keyserver network. 

As Vincent, I was just able to receive the keys with the given command
from the keyservers, no timeout. Could you please report, Anne, if the
server still times out and if you could solve your problem?

Of course, Vincent is right, you can also choose to trust the CRAN
servers and ignore the warning, that the key is not on your system.

Johannes


> Dirk
> 
> -- 
> Hell, there are no rules here - we're trying to accomplish something. 
>                                                   -- Thomas A. Edison
> 
> _______________________________________________
> R-SIG-Debian mailing list
> R-SIG-Debian at r-project.org
> https://stat.ethz.ch/mailman/listinfo/r-sig-debian

-- 
Dr. Johannes Ranke                 jranke at uni-bremen.de     Key ID: F649AF90
UFT Bremen, Leobenerstr. 1         +49 421 218 8971
D-28359 Bremen                     http://www.uft.uni-bremen.de/chemie/ranke
